# Enable GitHub Discussions

## Context

The foundation phase requires a public space for broader debate, process
questions, and early idea shaping — separate from structured proposals and
tasks. `docs/operating-model.md` already lists GitHub Discussions as the
intended tool.

## Work required

1. Open **Settings > General > Features > Discussions** in
   [zebadee2kk/project-atlas-foundation](https://github.com/zebadee2kk/project-atlas-foundation/settings).
2. Select **Set up discussions**.
3. Make **Discussions** visible in the repository tabs.

## Reference

Full step-by-step setup and category mapping (announcements, ideas,
general Q&A, proposals) are defined in [`docs/discussions-setup.md`](docs/discussions-setup.md).

## Definition of done

- The **Discussions** tab is visible in the repository.
- All four categories are created exactly as mapped in `docs/discussions-setup.md`.
- An inaugural **Proposals** post is pinned, establishing the expected format.
- Moderation settings are reviewed and at least one maintainer has admin rights.
- `docs/launch-checklist.md` item "Enable GitHub Discussions" is checked.
